title: Replace catastrophic route regex with a linear parser
prompt: RouteSpecParser validates dot-separated lowercase ASCII route segments. Its nested-quantifier regular expression has catastrophic backtracking on rejected inputs, which is measured deterministically through CountingCharSequence rather than wall-clock timing. Replace the regex implementation with an explicit, single-pass linear parser. Preserve the public API, unmodifiable returned segment lists, every currently accepted and rejected spelling, and zero-based SyntaxException error offsets. A valid input consists of one or more non-empty [a-z]+ segments separated by single dots. Preserve the operation instrumentation: every character inspection must go through CountingCharSequence, a successful parse reports one inspection per input character, rejection at an invalid character reports one inspection through that character, EOF-only syntax errors report one inspection per input character, and an attempted inspection beyond the positive operation limit throws OperationLimitExceededException with that limit. Do not modify the protected tests or raise/bypass the operation budget. Run `bash run-tests.sh`; verification must remain offline and deterministic.
